# UC-11.2.24 · SharePoint Online Bulk File Download Anomaly (Short Window)

## Description

A user downloading far more SharePoint files than peers in a one-hour window may indicate mass exfiltration, compromised credentials, or misconfigured sync clients.

## Value

Provides a statistical alternative to static thresholds so SOCs catch insiders and attackers without tuning per-department baselines manually.

## Implementation

Adjust the minimum floor (`200`) to tenant size. Exclude known migration or eDiscovery service principals via lookups. For very large tenants, run on summary data or increase window to 4h. Validate `Operation` remains `FileDownloaded` after Microsoft schema updates.

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload=SharePoint Operation="FileDownloaded" earliest=-1h
| eval actor=coalesce(UserId, UserPrincipalName, userId, "")
| stats count by actor
| eventstats perc95(count) as p95
| eval threshold=max(200, ceil(5*p95))
| where count > threshold
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (actor × download count), timechart of total downloads, single value (flagged actors).
